attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies entirely on leadout positioning rather than attacking. Wins from reduced bunch sprints through superior timing and patience, waiting for opponents to fade in final 200m.
team_tactics: Heavily dependent on high-quality leadout trains to deliver him into final kilometer. When leadout dissolves or fails (Alpecin-Fenix 2022, multiple Giro instances), rarely able to self-position effectively for contention.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with reduced peloton numbers suit him best. Exposed on any uphill gradients in finales and struggles when pace-making begins 40km+ from finish, getting dropped despite domestique support.
vulnerability: Lacks peak raw speed against top-tier sprinters even with perfect positioning. Dropped easily on sustained pace-making or uphill sprint finishes. Cannot compensate when leadout fails in final kilometer.
key_patterns: Excellent positioning but insufficient top-end speed documented across 4 observations spanning 2019-2022
Relies on timing and patience over power, waiting for premature efforts to fade
Gets dropped on any sustained pace 40km+ out or uphill finishes (3 observations)
Heavily leadout-dependent; rarely converts without perfect train delivery into final kilometer
Record 16 Giro podiums before first win suggests consistent positioning but speed deficit
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 observations show declining form with leadout failures and being dropped earlier in stages compared to 2020-2021 when he managed wins
